messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1773769802-kpEDsORXlLZXLNbd7z26","object":"chat.completion","created":1773769802,"model":"openai/gpt-5.4-nano-20260317","provider":"OpenAI","system_fingerprint":null,"choices":[{"index":0,"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{\n  \"svg\": \"<svg xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\" width=\\\"512\\\" height=\\\"512\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 512 512\\\">\\n  <rect x=\\\"0\\\" y=\\\"0\\\" width=\\\"512\\\" height=\\\"512\\\" fill=\\\"#ffffff\\\"/>\\n\\n  <!-- Outer frame -->\\n  <rect x=\\\"60\\\" y=\\\"60\\\" width=\\\"392\\\" height=\\\"392\\\" rx=\\\"18\\\" ry=\\\"18\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#94a3b8\\\" stroke-width=\\\"6\\\"/>\\n\\n  <!-- Labyrinth path (corridor/walls) -->\\n  <path d=\\\"M90 110 L180 110 L180 180 L260 180 L260 130 L330 130 L330 230 L290 230 L290 280 L360 280 L360 240 L410 240 L410 320 L340 320 L340 360 L270 360 L270 310 L230 310 L230 390 L310 390 L310 430 L420 430\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#e5e7eb\\\" stroke-width=\\\"36\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"/>\\n  <path d=\\\"M90 110 L180 110 L180 180 L260 180 L260 130 L330 130 L330 230 L290 230 L290 280 L360 280 L360 240 L410 240 L410 320 L340 320 L340 360 L270 360 L270 310 L230 310 L230 390 L310 390 L310 430 L420 430\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#0f172a\\\" stroke-width=\\\"28\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"/>\\n\\n  <!-- Entry/exit hints (no text) -->\\n  <circle cx=\\\"90\\\" cy=\\\"110\\\" r=\\\"12\\\" fill=\\\"#0f172a\\\"/>\\n  <circle cx=\\\"420\\\" cy=\\\"430\\\" r=\\\"12\\\" fill=\\\"#0f172a\\\"/>\\n</svg>\"\n}","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Designing maze walls**\n\nI'm thinking about how to create a labyrinth-like design. Instead of just trails, I want to draw walls using parallel lines, but that feels complex. It might be simpler to represent walls as a series of segments without fills. I'll base them on an 8x8 grid, creating six corridors for the maze. I'll plan to draw outer walls and internal walls, ensuring some openings to keep it true to a labyrinth style.**Creating a consistent maze design**\n\nI'm visualizing the maze as walls made up of lines, where gaps represent openings. For a consistent layout, I need specific wall placements. I’ll draw vertical walls at x = 140 and x = 220, and horizontal walls at y = 140 and y = 220, with specific endpoints for each. To connect passages from the top-left to the bottom-right, I might simplify things by drawing a corridor path with a thick stroke and shadow instead of just walls.**Designing a labyrinth path**\n\nI'm thinking about making a labyrinth using a single winding path drawn with a thick stroke. This seems like the easiest approach without any text. I’ll create a path that has a slight fill and maybe add faint inner boundaries for some depth. I'll set the stroke width to 24 with round caps and joins, and add a thin outline to define the corridors. The path will start at (100,90) and need to wind down to (412,420) in a continuous S-like design.**Creating polyline points for the maze**\n\nI'm working on crafting polyline points for the design. I've laid out a series of points starting from (110,110) and moving in various directions, but it doesn't end at the bottom-right. I realize I need to adjust the path to create a loop and aim for a more expansive design. My new goal is to end at (420,420), so I'll start at (100,90) and plan the route to go toward the center for a smooth finish.**Crafting the labyrinth path**\n\nI'm working on defining a polyline for the labyrinth, starting from (90,90) and aiming to end at (420,420). I want to create a winding path, so I've laid out several points, but I think I need more bends to truly achieve that labyrinth look. I plan to add additional turns and ensure the path weaves without too many self-intersections. message.reasoning:
**Designing maze walls**

I'm thinking about how to create a labyrinth-like design. Instead of just trails, I want to draw walls using parallel lines, but that feels complex. It might be simpler to represent walls as a series of segments without fills. I'll base them on an 8x8 grid, creating six corridors for the maze. I'll plan to draw outer walls and internal walls, ensuring some openings to keep it true to a labyrinth style.**Creating a consistent maze design**

I'm visualizing the maze as walls made up of lines, where gaps represent openings. For a consistent layout, I need specific wall placements. I’ll draw vertical walls at x = 140 and x = 220, and horizontal walls at y = 140 and y = 220, with specific endpoints for each. To connect passages from the top-left to the bottom-right, I might simplify things by drawing a corridor path with a thick stroke and shadow instead of just walls.**Designing a labyrinth path**

I'm thinking about making a labyrinth using a single winding path drawn with a thick stroke. This seems like the easiest approach without any text. I’ll create a path that has a slight fill and maybe add faint inner boundaries for some depth. I'll set the stroke width to 24 with round caps and joins, and add a thin outline to define the corridors. The path will start at (100,90) and need to wind down to (412,420) in a continuous S-like design.**Creating polyline points for the maze**

I'm working on crafting polyline points for the design. I've laid out a series of points starting from (110,110) and moving in various directions, but it doesn't end at the bottom-right. I realize I need to adjust the path to create a loop and aim for a more expansive design. My new goal is to end at (420,420), so I'll start at (100,90) and plan the route to go toward the center for a smooth finish.**Crafting the labyrinth path**

I'm working on defining a polyline for the labyrinth, starting from (90,90) and aiming to end at (420,420). I want to create a winding path, so I've laid out several points, but I think I need more bends to truly achieve that labyrinth look. I plan to add additional turns and ensure the path weaves without too many self-intersections. For visual appeal, I want a white background with dark, thick walls to represent the path and maybe include a subtle shadow for depth.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
